title: Monitor a Live Log File
scenario: A background service is writing events to /var/log/service/events.log. Your job is to monitor the log in real time and capture the moment a DEPLOY_SUCCESS event appears.
symptoms: Live log file being written by a background service
Need to detect a specific DEPLOY_SUCCESS event as it appears
mission: Use head and tail to preview the log, monitor it live with tail -f, capture the DEPLOY_SUCCESS event, and then capture the final tail of the log.

hints.title: Preview a File
hints.content: Preview a file: Use `head -5 /var/log/service/events.log > ~/log_preview.txt` to capture the first lines.

hints.title: Live Monitoring
hints.content: Live monitoring: Run `tail -f /var/log/service/events.log` and watch the output. When you see DEPLOY_SUCCESS, use Ctrl+C to stop.

hints.title: Capturing a Specific Event
hints.content: Capturing a specific event: Use `grep -m 1 'DEPLOY_SUCCESS' <(tail -f /var/log/service/events.log) > ~/deploy_event.txt` to automatically stop when the event appears.

hints.title: Last N Lines
hints.content: Last N lines: Use `tail -20 /var/log/service/events.log > ~/log_tail.txt` after the event has appeared.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/log_tail.txt exists and has at least 5 lines
